text.title: tracker-linear-agent-as-assignee Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for tracker-linear-agent-as-assignee. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The agent MUST NOT begin work on a Linear issue unless it received the matching IssueAssigned webhook with assignee.id equal to its own Linear user id within the last poll window. The agent MUST post one checkpoint comment within two minutes of acceptance, additional checkpoints at every state transition (started, blocked, ready-for-review), and exactly one final comment containing the PR URL when it opens the PR. Background work without a corresponding comment fails the audit and the run aborts.
rule.rationale: Linear's product contract treats the assignee as the primary owner; the comment cadence makes the agent's work visible at the same surface humans use, so reviewers do not have to leave Linear to track progress and the activity log captures every state transition under the agent's own identity.

rule.statement: The agent MUST connect to Linear MCP under its own bot-user OAuth token with role Member or below in every project it touches. The agent MUST be denied workspace-admin scope, MUST NOT use the human assignee's token, and MUST be removed from any project where its declared scope (see templates/agent-identity.yaml) does not list tracker.issue.comment and tracker.issue.update_status. Token issuance MUST log the (agent_id, project_id, scope) triple to the audit sink.
rule.rationale: Atlassian's "agents respect permissions, project configurations, workflows, and audit trails" articulates the invariant Linear also enforces: a runtime that can do more than its assignee can is a privilege-escalation primitive, and shared admin tokens defeat the per-action audit attribution that the agent-as-assignee model is built to provide.
